2605.03050.title: Evaluating Reasoning Models for Queries with Presuppositions
2605.03050.paper_summary: Millions of users turn to AI models for their information needs. It is conceivable that a large number of user queries contain assumptions that may be factually inaccurate. Prior work notes that large language models (LLMs) often fail to challenge such erroneous assumptions, and can reinforce users' misinformed opinions. However, given the recent advances, especially in model's reasoning capabilities, we revisit whether large reasoning models (LRMs) can reason about the underlying assumptions and respond to user queries appropriately. We construct queries with varying degrees of presuppositions spanning health, science, and general knowledge, and use it to evaluate several widely-deployed models When compared to non-reasoning models, we find that reasoning models achieve a slightly higher accuracy (2-11%), but they still fail to challenge a large fraction (26-42%) of false presuppositions. Further, reasoning models remain susceptible to how strongly the presupposition is expressed.
2605.03050.paper_summary_zh: 數百萬用戶轉向 AI 模型以滿足他們的信息需求。可以想見，大量用戶查詢中包含的假設可能在事實上是不準確的。先前的研究指出，大型語言模型（LLMs）經常未能挑戰這些錯誤的假設，並且可能加強用戶的錯誤觀點。然而，考慮到最近的進展，特別是在模型推理能力方面，我們重新檢視大型推理模型（LRMs）是否能夠推理潛在的假設並適當地回應用戶查詢。我們構建了包含不同程度前提的查詢，涵蓋健康、科學和一般知識，並用它來評估幾個廣泛部署的模型。與非推理模型相比，我們發現推理模型的準確率略高（2-11%），但它們仍然未能挑戰大量（26-42%）的錯誤前提。此外，推理模型仍然容易受到前提表達強度的影響。
2605.03050.author: Rose Sathyanathan et.al.
2605.03050.authors: Rose Sathyanathan, Kinshuk Vasisht, Danish Pruthi
